items.title: Free Pick for Wednesday, July 22: Seattle Mariners ML (-155)
items.html:
Cincinnati Reds @ Seattle Mariners
Pick: Seattle Mariners ML (-155) · 65.1% of 10,000 sims · edge +4.3 pts vs the -155 price
A strong play, but not our Play of the Day: the top-confidence plays go to premium members. This one is free and in full.

The mound tilts this game. Emerson Hancock brings a 3.17 ERA and 1.01 WHIP over 99.1 innings against Brady Singer at 4.56 (1.41 WHIP). A 1.4-run ERA gap, weighted over the ~5.5 innings a starter covers, moves real win probability before anyone swings. Baserunners are kindling: the 1.01-vs-1.41 WHIP spread decides whose innings stay quiet.

The clubs, the park, the count. Seattle Mariners (51-51) score 4.04 and allow 3.85 runs a game; Cincinnati Reds (46-54) score 4.18 and allow 4.83. In a run-suppressing park (factor 0.92), 10,000 simulations land on a 2.7–4.2 average score, with Seattle Mariners winning 65.1% of them, a fair price of -186.

The market price is the point. The consensus line is -155 against our -186 fair, a +4.3-point edge and +7.0% EV per unit at the listed price. Rule 8 checked the disagreement with the de-vigged market: 6.5 points, inside the 12-point cap: a disagreement, not a delusion. Quarter-Kelly would size this at 2.7% of bankroll; the tier framework governs it to 1u. The governor beats the gas pedal.

Breaker sheet: Every automated breaker passed at full size. The full check log is printed below, including the passes.

Bottom line: Seattle Mariners at -155 carries a 4.3-point model edge at 1u. If the line steams past our -186 fair before first pitch, the edge is gone; pass without regret. Passing is a position too.

See the full board and the running record →

items.title: Free Pick for Thursday, July 23: Tampa Bay Rays ML (-110)
items.html:
Tampa Bay Rays @ Toronto Blue Jays
Pick: Tampa Bay Rays ML (-110) · 61.1% of 10,000 sims · edge +8.7 pts vs the -110 price
A strong play, but not our Play of the Day: the top-confidence plays go to premium members. This one is free and in full.

The mound tilts this game. Ian Seymour brings a 4.79 ERA and 1.17 WHIP over 67.2 innings against Shane Bieber at 5.70 (1.73 WHIP). A 0.9-run ERA gap, weighted over the ~5.5 innings a starter covers, moves real win probability before anyone swings. Baserunners are kindling: the 1.17-vs-1.73 WHIP spread decides whose innings stay quiet.

The clubs, the park, the count. Tampa Bay Rays (59-42) score 4.53 and allow 4.22 runs a game; Toronto Blue Jays (46-56) score 3.94 and allow 4.56. In a neutral park (factor 1.00), 10,000 simulations land on a 5.4–3.9 average score, with Tampa Bay Rays winning 61.1% of them, a fair price of -157.

The market price is the point. The consensus line is -110 against our -157 fair, a +8.7-point edge and +16.7% EV per unit at the listed price. Rule 8 checked the disagreement with the de-vigged market: 10.5 points, inside the 12-point cap: a disagreement, not a delusion. Quarter-Kelly would size this at 4.6% of bankroll; the tier framework governs it to 0.5u. The governor beats the gas pedal.

Breaker sheet: Rule 4 cut the allocation over a limited-workload starter.

Bottom line: Tampa Bay Rays at -110 carries a 8.7-point model edge at 0.5u. If the line steams past our -157 fair before first pitch, the edge is gone; pass without regret. Passing is a position too.
